title: Day 5: Simulation & GR2
concepts: Pacing simulator replays historical auction logs with counterfactual bids to estimate delivery and cost under new pacing policies without live traffic risk.
Experiment design for auction changes needs careful randomization unit – user, request, or advertiser – to avoid interference and spillover bias.
Counterfactual measurement uses replay or synthetic control to estimate what would have happened under alternative auction parameters.
Scaling from pilot to production requires staged rollout, guardrail metrics on user experience and advertiser value, and rollback criteria.
DPA test surfaces allow controlled evaluation of generative auction and OVAR changes on limited inventory before broad launch.

quiz.question: Why use a pacing simulator instead of live A/B only?
options: Simulators allow safe counterfactual exploration of many policy variants on historical data before risking live budget
quiz.explanation: Replay simulation tests policy changes offline at scale, reducing risk and speeding iteration before live experiment.

quiz.front: What is counterfactual measurement in auction experiments?
quiz.back: Estimating outcome under alternative auction parameters that were not actually applied – via replay simulation, synthetic control groups, or instrumental variables – to attribute causal effect of change beyond simple A/B difference.

quiz.statement: Scaling pilot to production should happen in one big switch for speed.
quiz.why: False – staged rollout with guardrails allows detection of regressions in user experience or advertiser value before full impact. Rollback plan essential.

quiz.question: Choosing randomization unit in auction experiment matters because:
options: Wrong unit causes interference – e.g., user-level randomization can leak across auctions within same request, biasing results
quiz.explanation: Auction interference violates SUTVA – careful unit choice (request, user, advertiser cluster) reduces spillover and gives valid causal estimates.

quiz.statement: DPA test surfaces are used for controlled evaluation of new auction mechanisms on limited inventory.
quiz.why: True – Dynamic Product Ads or similar controlled surfaces provide safer initial testbed before expanding to broad auction traffic.
